methods.method_name: Surface Sediment Sample Collection and Preparation
methods.description: Procedure for collecting surface sediment samples from marginal marine environments, washing them to remove fine particles, and drying them for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: Surface sediment samples were collected from marginal marine environments (marsh and intertidal mudflats) at Swanwick and Warsash near Southampton
steps.searchable_summary: Collect surface sediment samples from specified locations and environments

steps.description: washed on a 63 pm sieve
steps.searchable_summary: Wash sediment samples using a 63 µm sieve

steps.description: and dried at 50°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry washed sediment samples at 50°C

methods.description: Procedure for preparing and analyzing specimens (likely foraminifera shells) using SEM for imaging and EDS for semiquantitative elemental analysis.

steps.description: For scanning electron microscopy (SEM), the specimens were mounted onto aluminium stubs
steps.searchable_summary: Mount specimens onto SEM stubs

steps.description: and coated with carbon
steps.searchable_summary: Coat mounted specimens with carbon for SEM

steps.description: before introduction into the vacuum chamber of the Jeol JSM-6400 scanning electron microscope with a Tracor Series I1 energy dispersive X-ray analysis system.
steps.searchable_summary: Load coated specimens into SEM vacuum chamber

steps.description: Photographs were taken so that a point correlation with EDS analysis could be made.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire SEM images (photographs) of specimens

steps.description: Semiquantitative point analysis was performed on individual grains and the results collected.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform semiquantitative EDS point analysis on individual grains and collect results

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing the molecular vibrations of selected specimens (likely foraminifera shells) using a Raman microprobe.

steps.description: Selected specimens were placed loose on a glass slide
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare specimens by placing them on a glass slide for Raman analysis

steps.description: and analysed using the Raman laser microprobe in the 10&1000cm~' region of the spectrum.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire Raman spectra from specimens using a laser microprobe in the specified spectral range

steps.description: In this study, integration times of IG300 s with a single accumulation were used with each analysis taking about 6 min to complete.
steps.searchable_summary: Set Raman acquisition parameters (integration time, accumulations)

steps.description: Calibration was achieved through monitoring plasma lines from the laser and through the use of quartz and sulphur standards.
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate Raman spectrometer using plasma lines and standards (quartz, sulphur)

steps.description: The spectra were processed and manipulated with ISA PRISM software.
steps.searchable_summary: Process and manipulate Raman spectra using ISA PRISM software

steps.description: The peak position was determined at maximum intensity
steps.searchable_summary: Find peak positions in Raman spectra at maximum intensity

steps.description: and the line-width measured at half-maximum intensity (Roberts et ul., 1995).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure peak line-widths (FWHM) in Raman spectra

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing the infrared absorption properties of sections of foraminifera tests using FTIR spectroscopy.

steps.description: Sections of foraminifera test were placed on a salt window for analysis by FTIR.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare specimens by placing sections on a salt window for FTIR analysis

steps.description: The spectral range was 4000-600 ern-'.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire FTIR spectra from specimens in the specified spectral range

steps.description: Background acquisition took place after each acquisition to remove additional components from the spectra.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire background FTIR spectra after each sample acquisition

steps.description: Background acquisition took place after each acquisition to remove additional components from the spectra.
steps.searchable_summary: Subtract background from FTIR spectra to remove additional components

steps.description: Noise levels were monitored throughout the experiments and were found to be very low.
steps.searchable_summary: Monitor noise levels during FTIR acquisition

methods.description: Procedure for identifying mineral phases in bulk sediment, heavy mineral, and clay fractions using X-ray diffraction.

steps.description: X-Ray diffraction (Philips) analyses were performed on sediments collected from the same area as the foraminifera.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sediment samples for XRD analysis from the specified area

steps.description: A 1.00 mass of bulk sediment was ground and the resultant powder was scanned
steps.searchable_summary: Grind 1.00 g of bulk sediment sample to a powder

steps.description: the resultant powder was scanned from 2 to 70° 20 at a scanning velocity of 1.2"/min using Ni-filtered CoK cv radiation.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire XRD pattern from sediment powder using specified parameters

steps.description: Peaks were identified using the associated computer software.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify peaks in the XRD pattern using associated software

steps.description: Additionally, heavy minerals and clays were separated from the sediment fraction
steps.searchable_summary: Separate heavy mineral and clay fractions from the sediment

steps.description: and analysed under the same conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire XRD patterns from separated heavy mineral and clay fractions under the same conditions
inputs: Same conditions as bulk sediment scan
